{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27529","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27529/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27529/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27529/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27529","id":2572475621,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86ZVNzl","number":27529,"title":"BUG: Invalid calculation of percentile","user":{"login":"george0st","id":95856749,"node_id":"U_kgDOBbaobQ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95856749?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st","html_url":"https://github.com/george0st","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-08T08:28:37Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T08:28:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

Calculation of percentile with method 'lower' does not work correctly, see sample:

- input list **[0.34, 0.24, 0.11, 0.21, 0.33]**
- order list **[0.11, 0.21, 0.24, 0.33, 0.34]**
- 70 percentile = **4.2** in case of method 'lower', the value will be **4** => expected output will be **0.33** but numpy returns value **0.24**

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np

sequence = [0.34, 0.24, 0.11, 0.21, 0.33]
print(f"Numpy: {np.percentile(sequence,70, method='lower')}")
```


### Error message:

```shell
It is without error, but output value is invalid
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.1.2 and 2.1.1
3.11.9 (tags/v3.11.9:de54cf5, Apr  2 2024, 10:12:12) [MSC v.1938 64 bit (AMD64)]

### Runtime Environment:

_No response_

### Context for the issue:

```python

print(f"Numpy: {np.percentile(sequence,70, method='lower')}")
```

```
lower:
NumPy method kept for backwards compatibility. Takes i as the interpolation point.

where:
The optional method parameter specifies the method to use when the desired percentile lies between two indexes i and j = i + 1
```

Problem

It will be useful to **generate warning** in case that code contains more plots and these plots need to set different x-scale (with xticks). In case of subplots with sharex='all', it will block to slow relevant x-label descriptions.

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, **sharex="all"**, squeeze=False, figsize=(15, 6))
    ...
    ax_cur.set_xticks(x_lst)


### Proposed solution

Generate warning in case of blocked functions see:

- subplots with sharex='all'
- and more plots with different setting of x-scale (with xticks)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

- subplots with sharex='all'
- and more plots with different setting of x-scale (with xticks)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/comments/1768310955","html_url":"https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124#issuecomment-1768310955","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/27124","id":1768310955,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABUios5pZkir","user":{"login":"george0st","id":95856749,"node_id":"U_kgDOBbaobQ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/95856749?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st","html_url":"https://github.com/george0st","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/george0st/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-10-18T12:07:08Z","updated_at":"2023-10-18T12:07:08Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I had a problem to see x-axis description for first subplot (I used Axes.set_xticks). See sample of code:

```
import matplotlib.axes
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

y_lst=[100,200,150,500]
x_lst=[2,4,8,16]

plt.style.use("bmh") #"ggplot" "seaborn-v0_8-poster"
fig, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, sharex="all", squeeze=False, figsize=(15, 6))
ax_cur: matplotlib.axes.Axes = ax[0][0]

plt.suptitle("Performance",weight='bold', fontsize=18, ha="center", va="top")
ax_cur.set_title("title", fontsize=14,ha="center", va="top")

ax_cur.plot(x_lst, y_lst, color='green', linestyle="-")
ax_cur.legend()
ax_cur.set_ylabel('RTF [calls/sec]')
ax_cur.set_xticks(x_lst)

plt.grid()
plt.show()
```
See the output:
![Figure_1](https://github.com/matplotlib/matplotlib/assets/95856749/ce1a1e3c-6d0e-457e-8fd5-3b20be118cdf)

I solved the issue based on  sharex="none", but it will be useful to generate warning for coders (only idea).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/comments/1768310955/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
